user_input: How do I reset my password?
reference: Go to the sign-in page, click 'Forgot password', enter your account email, and use the reset link (valid 30 minutes) to set a new password of at least 12 characters. If the email doesn't arrive in 5 minutes, check spam or contact support.

user_input: My password reset link expired, what do I do?
reference: Request a new link from the 'Forgot password' page. Reset links expire after 30 minutes and can only be used once; requesting a new one invalidates the previous.

user_input: How do I enable two-factor authentication?
reference: Go to Settings → Security → Two-Factor Authentication, scan the QR code with an authenticator app (Google Authenticator or Authy), confirm with the 6-digit code, and save your backup codes.

user_input: I lost my phone and don't have MFA backup codes.
reference: Contact support with proof of account ownership. They disable MFA after identity verification, which takes 1-2 business days. Backup codes would have been the fastest recovery path.

user_input: Where can I find past invoices?
reference: Past invoices are under Settings → Billing → Invoices as downloadable PDFs, including the billing period, plan, seats, taxes, and payment method.

user_input: What happens if my payment fails?
reference: We email the billing contact and retry after 3 and 7 days. After three failed attempts the account downgrades to Free but data is preserved for 90 days; re-adding a valid payment method resumes the account on the next successful charge.

user_input: How do I upgrade or downgrade my plan?
reference: From Settings → Billing → Plan. Upgrades take effect immediately with proration; downgrades take effect at the next renewal so paid features remain until the period ends.

user_input: How do I generate an API key?
reference: Generate API keys from Settings → API Keys. Keys are workspace-scoped, never expire unless revoked, and should be stored securely. Revoke instantly from the same page if one leaks.

user_input: What are the API rate limits per plan?
reference: Free is 60 requests/minute, Pro 600/minute, Enterprise custom. Rate-limited responses return HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header; use exponential backoff. Short bursts up to 2x are allowed.

user_input: How do I verify webhooks are from you?
reference: Verify the X-Signature header using your webhook secret. We retry failed deliveries up to 5 times over 24 hours, then disable the webhook until you fix it.

user_input: How do I export my data?
reference: From Settings → Data → Export. We produce a JSON or CSV archive within 24 hours and email a download link valid for 7 days. Exports include content and attachments but not audit logs older than 1 year.

user_input: Is SSO available and on which plan?
reference: SAML 2.0 SSO is available on the Enterprise plan. Configure it from Settings → Security → SSO by providing your IdP metadata URL or XML; test with a single user before enforcing it org-wide.
99.9% monthly uptime for paid plans. Status is published on the status page with real-time component health and incident history; maintenance windows are announced at least 72 hours in advance.
List endpoints return up to 100 items per page with a cursor for the next page. Use ?limit= up to 100 and ?cursor= from the previous response. Cursors are stable for 24 hours.

Permissions are role-based (Owner/Admin/Editor/Viewer), refined per project, and evaluated server-side on every action. Client-side hiding is not a security control; custom roles are available on Enterprise.
